headline: Anthropic's Fable 5 launch dominated the week as users contested its 2x price premium over Opus 4.8.
body: Anthropic's Fable 5 (also called Mythos 5) drew live tests and benchmarks against Opus 4.8 and GPT 5.5, with one $200-plan user reporting that Fable consumed 45% of a weekly quota in a single day. Anthropic separately reversed a policy that would have secretly limited Claude on frontier LLM development tasks, conceding it had wrongly traded off transparency. On the hardware and technique side, NVIDIA shipped Nemotron 3 in Nano, Super, and Ultra sizes, combining a hybrid Mamba-Transformer with latent mixture of experts and multitask token prediction, while Groq's Jonathan Ross framed cheaper inference through Jevons Paradox. LLMs and next-token prediction led the rotation and broke out alongside RLHF.

cited_items.title: Building with Anthropic's Fable 5 LIVE - Is It Really Worth the Hype?
Anthropic Walks Back Policy That Could Have ‘Sabotaged’ AI Researchers Using Claude
Tell HN: Anthropic's Fable model is too expensive
"An endless demand for compute" | Jonathan Ross, founder of Groq
